Fresh on the Menu

New at Kabab Culture

A few new arrivals we think you’ll love. Tap close to browse the full menu.

Juicy Angaarey Chicken Tikka

18.95

Charred outside, juicy within, with a slow-building spice. Angaarey means “embers” — and you’ll taste them.

Ajwaini Paneer Tikka

18.95

Fresh paneer and sweet peppers in carom-seed yogurt, grilled in the tandoor until smoky and golden.

Jalebi Caviar on Rabri

8.95

Tiny spheres of saffron-syrup “caviar” on a bed of slow-reduced rabri. A whimsical finale you won’t see coming.

North Indian · Tandoor · Kababs

Our menu is concise, and each dish has its own personality. Start with kababs & appetizers, follow with a curry or biryani, and finish with warm bread or rice.

Drinks Non-Veg Kababs Veg Kababs Non-Veg Mains Veg Mains Bread & Sides Dessert

Non-Alcoholic Drinks

Mango Lassi

6.95

Chilled mango blended with yogurt — sweet, creamy, and the perfect palate refresher.

Homemade Ginger Collins

8.95

Fresh ginger muddled with lemon juice and a touch of sugar, topped with sparkling soda — bright, spicy, and wonderfully refreshing.

Homemade Mojito

8.95

Garden-fresh mint leaves muddled with lemon juice and cane sugar, finished with soda — light, crisp, and cooling.

Soda

4.95

Club · Coke · Diet Coke · Sprite

Non-Vegetarian Kababs & Appetizers

All kababs are served with a side of chopped salad, sliced onions, and mint chutney.

Fish Tikka

29.95

Mahi Mahi fillets soaked in a vibrant blend of yogurt, turmeric, and coastal spices, then kissed by the tandoor for smoky, charred edges and a moist, flaky center.

Fish Amritsari

26.95

A beloved street-food classic from Amritsar — golden-fried catfish in a fiery chickpea batter spiked with carom seeds and red chili. Crunchy outside, tender within.

Chicken Malai Tikka

18.95

Tender chicken marinated in fresh cream, cashew paste, and mild spices, then grilled in the tandoor until silky smooth — delicate, buttery, and subtly spiced.

Juicy Angaarey Chicken Tikka

18.95

Boldly marinated chicken tenders — charred on the outside, juicy within, with a slow-building spice that lingers. Angaarey means “embers,” and you’ll taste them.

Chicken Reshmi Seekh Kabab

17.95

Finely minced chicken blended with herbs, green chili, and aromatic spices, hand-molded onto skewers and grilled to juicy, silken perfection.

Chicken Chop

21.95

French-trimmed chicken drumettes marinated in the chef’s signature blend, roasted in the tandoor until caramelized and smoky.

Lucknow Style ‘Galouti’ Kabab

29.95

Born in the royal kitchens of Lucknow for a Nawab who could no longer chew, these melt-in-the-mouth lamb patties are perfumed with over two dozen spices and served on warm mini bread.

Chef Casley ki PasliChef's SpecialChef’s Special Lamb Chops

44.95

Our chef’s crown jewel — thick-cut lamb chops steeped overnight in a proprietary house marinade, then grilled to a blushing, smoky char.

Vegetarian Kababs & Appetizers

Beet Root Kabab

14.95

Vibrant, earthy beetroot blended with spiced lentils and pan-griddled to a crisp crust — a jewel-red patty with earthy sweetness and gentle heat.

Patte Ki Chaat

15.95

This street-food sensation features crisp fried leaves (patte) coated in a special batter — tangy, sweet, spicy, and crunchy all at once.

Tandoori Stuffed Mushroom Shaslik

15.95

Plump whole mushrooms filled with a herbed spinach and cream cheese blend, skewered with colorful peppers, and charred in the tandoor for a smoky, molten center.

Gobhi 65

14.95

Cauliflower florets twice-fried to an addictive crunch and tossed with curry leaves, mustard seeds, and fiery red chili.

Dahi Ke Kabab

16.95

Strained yogurt blended with herbs, shaped into delicate dumplings, and fried until golden.

Ajwaini Paneer Tikka

18.95

Fresh-made paneer and sweet bell peppers marinated in carom-seed spiced yogurt, then grilled in the tandoor until smoky and golden.

Non-Vegetarian Main Courses

Served with a side of rice.

Andhra Lamb Curry

26.95

Slow-cooked lamb in a blazing Andhra gravy of Guntur red chilies, curry leaves, and tamarind. Deep, fierce, and built for heat lovers.

Fisherman’s Shrimp Curry

25.95

Plump shrimp simmered in a bold coastal gravy of slow-cooked onions, sun-ripe tomatoes, ginger-garlic, and fresh coconut milk.

Butter Chicken (Our Version)

22.95

Our house take on the world’s most beloved Indian dish — tandoor-kissed chicken folded into a velvety tomato and butter sauce, gently spiced and finished with cream. Call it what you like; just order it.

Chettinad Egg Curry

17.95

Hard-boiled eggs in a fierce, aromatic Chettinad gravy of freshly ground spices, curry leaves, and coconut — bold, smoky, and unmistakably South Indian.

Chicken Xacutti

18.95

A fiery Goan classic — bone-in chicken in a dark, richly spiced coconut gravy of toasted whole spices, dried chili, and tamarind. Complex and unmistakably coastal.

Methi Chicken Curry

18.95

Tender chicken braised in a fragrant gravy laced with fresh fenugreek (methi) leaves — the herb’s slightly bitter, herbal edge perfectly balancing the richness of the sauce.

Kadai Chicken

18.95

Chunks of chicken stir-cooked in a wok (kadai) with charred peppers, onions, and whole spices — the sizzle and smoke of the open flame locked into every bite.

Goat Biryani

24.95

Slow-cooked bone-in goat and aged Basmati rice layered with caramelised onions, saffron, and whole spices, then sealed in a dough-lidded pot to trap every whisper of steam and fragrance. Opened tableside.

Chicken Dum Biryani

20.95

Bone-in chicken and fragrant Basmati rice cooked together over low heat in a dough-sealed pot — the slow steam (dum) coaxes the spices deep into every grain. Aromatic, satisfying, complete.

Goat Curry

24.95

North Indian home cooking at its finest — bone-in goat simmered for hours in a deep, silky onion-tomato gravy until the meat is fall-tender and the sauce richly layered with spice.

Laal Maas

24.95

The legendary red-meat curry of Rajasthan — goat slow-cooked in a blazing sauce of mathania red chili, garlic, and whole spices. Laal Maas means “red meat,” and the color tells you everything. Not for the faint of heart.

Vegetarian Main Courses

Served with a side of rice.

Matar Paneer Bhurjee

21.95

Fresh paneer crumbled and sautéed with sweet green peas, tomatoes, and a punchy blend of whole spices — a boldly seasoned scramble with rustic, home-kitchen energy.

Lehsooni Tadka Sag with Onion Pakora

15.95

Silky braised spinach finished with a sizzling garlic and cumin tempering, crowned with a crispy fried onion pakora.

Mango Malai Kofta

21.95

Our seasonal riff on the classic — soft, stuffed paneer and vegetable dumplings floating in a lush cream sauce brightened with a hint of mango.

Paneer Butter Masala

19.95

Pillowy paneer in a velvety sauce of slow-roasted tomatoes, cashews, and butter, finished with cream and a whisper of cardamom.

Channa Masala

16.95

Chickpeas slow-cooked in a bold, tangy tomato and onion masala with whole spices and a squeeze of tamarind. Hearty, warming, and deeply satisfying.

Toor Masur ‘Double Tadka’ Dal

15.95

A blend of two lentils (toor and masur) cooked to a smooth, homestyle consistency, hit twice with a sizzling tempering of mustard seeds, dried chili, and garlic, and brightened with tamarind.

Jackfruit ‘Kathal’ Biryani

19.95

Young jackfruit — prized for its remarkably meat-like texture — marinated in biryani spices and layered with fragrant Basmati in a sealed pot. A bold, hearty centerpiece that surprises even non-vegetarians.

Makhani Soy Curry

18.95

Soya chaap — a tender, protein-rich soy preparation — simmered in our house makhani gravy of roasted tomatoes, butter, and cream. All the comfort of the classic, entirely plant-based.

Dal Makhani

15.95

Whole black lentils and red kidney beans simmered overnight with butter and cream until impossibly rich and velvety — the definitive dal of North India, and an institution for good reason.

Achari Baigan

15.95

Tender eggplant cooked with a bracing blend of pickle-inspired spices — mustard seeds, fennel, nigella, and dried chili — for a tangy, pungent, and deeply savoury result. A bold vegetarian dish with personality.

Saag Paneer

19.95

Golden cubes of fresh paneer nestled in a slow-braised spinach gravy perfumed with garlic and warming spices. A North Indian staple, done the slow way.

Kadai Paneer

19.95

Fresh paneer and colorful peppers stir-cooked in a wok (kadai) with hand-ground spices — coriander, dried chili, black pepper — for a dry, bold curry with wonderful texture and heat.

Jeera Aloo

15.95

Potatoes pan-roasted with whole cumin seeds, green chili, ginger, and fresh coriander until golden and fragrant.

Over Time!

You can’t leave with salt in your mouth.

‘Oye Teri’ Kulfi

9.95

Dense, slow-frozen Indian ice cream made in the old style — richer and creamier than anything machine-churned. Ask your server for today’s exotic house flavors.

Jalebi Caviar on Rabri

8.95

A playful reinvention of the jalebi — tiny spheres of saffron-syrup “caviar” nestled on a bed of slow-reduced rabri (clotted cream). A whimsical finale you won’t see coming.

Gulab Jamun

Plain 6.95
w/ Rabri 8.95

Soft milk-solid dumplings soaked in cardamom and rose-water syrup until perfectly saturated — served warm on their own or with a luxurious pour of house-made rabri.

Kesar Ras Malai

10.95

Delicate fresh-cheese patties poached in sweetened milk perfumed with saffron (kesar) and cardamom — light, floral, and elegantly restrained.

Moong Dal Halwa

12.95

A Rajasthani winter heirloom — split yellow lentils slow-roasted in ghee with sugar until they caramelize into a deeply nutty, fudge-like halwa. Finished with pistachios and almonds. Warming and wonderfully indulgent.

Ice Cream
